articles.title: Gordon puts England ahead against Argentina
articles.summary: Anthony Gordon puts England 1-0 up against Argentina in the 2026 World Cup semi-final at the Atlanta Stadium.

articles.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
articles.summary: The safety board confirmed Tesla's account of the crash, which the company shared days after it happened last month.

articles.title: Ukraine strikes 20 Russian vessels

articles.title: The history of the US and Iraq’s complicated relationship
articles.summary: As Iraq’s prime minister visits Washington seeking deeper economic ties.

articles.title: England and Argentina national anthems drowned out
articles.summary: Both England and Argentina's national anthems are drowned out by crowd noise, before the teams kick off their 2026 World Cup semi-final at the Atlanta Stadium.

articles.title: Protests in Paris, Gaza for release of Hussam Abu Safia

articles.title: Daniel Ek’s body-scanning startup Neko Health raises another $700M
articles.summary: Neko Health has developed proprietary body-scanning technology, which it couples with bloodwork, to assess a person's health.

articles.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
articles.summary: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

articles.title: Fourteen-year-old boy charged over alleged plot targeting London mosques
articles.summary: Suspect is charged with 'preparation of terrorist acts - linked to extreme right-wing terrorism', say British police.

articles.title: Syrian authorities arrest ex-officer accused of chemical weapons crimes

articles.title: European Athletics moves to curb on-screen sexualisation of female athletes
articles.summary: The guidelines are based on feedback from athletes who said certain camera shots can cause discomfort and distractions.

articles.title: Palestinian homes sealed off by Israeli military restrictions

articles.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II

articles.title: Salary information to be shown on job ads under new laws

articles.title: West Indies beat Ireland to win ODI series 3-0

articles.summary: US President Donald Trump vowed to strike Iran's bridges and power plants next week if the country does not return to talks.

articles.title: US intelligence director pick refuses to acknowledge Trump loss in 2020
articles.summary: Jay Clayton, often in circuitous fashion, avoided saying Joe Biden won 2020 race during his confirmation hearing.

articles.title: Benn to face WBC champion Garcia on 12 September

articles.title: Lebanon and Israel end sixth round of US-brokered talks

articles.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
articles.summary: Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with several other sensitive categories, suggesting Apple is taking a more curated approach to these ads.

articles.title: SpaceX share price drops below stock market debut
articles.summary: Share trading in Elon Musk’s rocket company has been volatile since it went public a month ago.

articles.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
articles.summary: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

articles.title: Headers caused Stiles' brain disease, coroner finds
articles.summary: England 1966 World Cup winner Nobby Stiles died with a condition caused by heading footballs, his inquest concludes.

articles.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model, Inkling
articles.summary: It's the company's first public proof point after a year and a half spent building AI infrastructure largely out of public view.

articles.title: Gas tanker explodes in fire near army barracks in Lebanon

articles.title: Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data

articles.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations

articles.title: Inside Ode with Anthropic, the startup betting AI services are the future of enterprise
articles.summary: Can a handful of engineers really do the work of an army of consultants? That’s the bet behind Ode with Anthropic — the joint venture dedicated to embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs and others. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca Bellan sits down with Ode’s leaders Chris Taylor and Eddie Siegel, who founded Fractional AI, […]
